medications: Hydroxyzine 25 mg as needed for acute anxiety/sleep
Triamcinolone 0.1% ointment short courses for eczema flares
Cetirizine 10 mg daily during allergy season
health.mental_health: Since the baby was born, Andres has had heightened anxiety, irritability, and rumination about making mistakes at work and at home. Sleep fragmentation from night feedings worsens symptoms. He’s in therapy focused on anxiety management and adjusting to new-parent responsibilities while supporting his partner’s postpartum recovery.
recent_visits: Primary care visit for anxiety medication follow-up and sleep issues (late January)
Dermatology appointment for eczema flare on hands from frequent washing (early February)
Urgent care visit for a stubborn facial dermatitis flare after trying a new skincare product (December)

finance.debt: About $18,000 in student loans from a paralegal program and $3,200 on a credit card that grew after the baby’s birth (diapers, formula, a car repair, and unpaid time off). He and his partner are behind on one utility bill and are trying to avoid overdrafts.
investments: Employer 401(k) with small biweekly contributions (paused temporarily)
recent_transactions: Daycare deposit and first month payment
Purchase of a breast pump replacement parts kit and postpartum supplies for partner
Co-pays for therapy and dermatology visits
Large Target run for diapers, wipes, and formula
Auto repair for a failing alternator
Payment plan set up with a hospital billing office for delivery-related charges under partner’s name

relationships: Partner: Maya Thompson (23), recovering from postpartum complications; they’re co-parenting and juggling sleep shifts
Infant son: Eli Zhang (born this winter)
Mother: Lihua Zhang (lives in St. Paul; helps with meals twice a week)
Close friend: Jordan Kline (college friend; checks in by text, occasional late-night calls)
Work mentor: Renee Alvarez (senior paralegal; supportive but expects reliability)
social.social_media_activity: Rarely posts publicly. Shares occasional baby photos to a private Instagram story for close friends/family and uses Reddit/Discord for skincare and new-parent tips under a pseudonym. Avoids discussing work online due to confidentiality.
private_conversations: Arguments with Maya about division of night feedings and returning to work before she feels ready
Confiding in Jordan about anxiety spikes and fear of being a bad parent
Discussing Maya’s postpartum recovery details and medical follow-ups (kept within family)
Texting his mom about needing short-term financial help for daycare and utilities

schedule.work_hours: Monday–Friday, typically 8:30 AM–5:30 PM with occasional late evenings during filing deadlines; limited flexibility due to court schedules
regular_appointments: Weekly telehealth therapy on Wednesdays at 7:30 PM
Pediatrician checkups and vaccinations (monthly in early infancy)
Daycare drop-off/pick-up (alternating with partner depending on her recovery)
upcoming_events: Family court filing deadline next Tuesday (expected late night at the office)
Eli’s two-month pediatric visit and vaccines
HR meeting about using remaining PTO and adjusting schedule (requested by Andres)
Partner’s postpartum follow-up appointment (Andres plans to attend)
schedule.daily_routine: Up at 5:30–6:00 AM for a feeding/diaper change, quick shower and skincare to manage dermatitis, commute by light rail or car depending on weather, workday focused on document prep and deadlines, home by early evening to take a feeding shift and do dishes/laundry, then tries to sleep in 2–3 hour blocks. Uses a shared calendar with Maya to coordinate appointments and naps.

professional.company: Mid-size Minneapolis litigation firm (civil and family law) that handles court filings and discovery-heavy cases
projects: Preparing discovery responses and document productions for a civil litigation matter
Drafting and filing motions, exhibits, and affidavits for family court cases
Coordinating deposition scheduling, subpoenas, and witness outreach
Managing an e-discovery database and privilege logs under attorney supervision
professional.performance_issues: He’s had a couple of near-misses with deadlines due to sleep deprivation and has made minor formatting/citation errors that required last-minute fixes. He’s anxious about being perceived as unreliable after requesting schedule accommodations and taking sporadic time off for pediatric and postpartum appointments.
workplace_relationships: Works closely with attorney Priya Desai, who is demanding about turnaround time but fair
Relies on mentor Renee Alvarez for procedural guidance; feels embarrassed asking for help repeatedly
Tension with a newer paralegal, Sam Whitaker, who has complained informally about Andres leaving on time for family responsibilities

hobbies: Pickup basketball at a neighborhood rec center (when sleep allows)
Cooking simple one-pan meals and meal prepping with his mom’s help
PC gaming late at night on weekends (short sessions)
Walking around Lake Nokomis with the stroller to calm the baby
habits: Skin routine twice daily (gentle cleanser, moisturizer, topical acne treatment) and wears cotton gloves at night during eczema flares
Drinks 2–3 coffees most workdays to cope with fatigue
Uses a mindfulness app for 10 minutes during lunch when anxiety spikes
Occasional nicotine vaping when stressed (tries to hide it from family and is attempting to quit)
Tracks baby feedings and sleep in an app shared with his partner
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a small one-bedroom apartment in South Minneapolis with Maya and the baby; they converted part of the living room into a nursery corner. Noise concerns and lack of space add stress, and they’re considering moving when finances allow.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Not strictly dietary-restricted but tries to eat high-protein snacks and easy meals. Limits spicy foods during dermatitis flares, drinks a lot of water, and relies on quick groceries (rotisserie chicken, frozen vegetables) due to time constraints.
